description_long: This is a rustic, two-rail wooden fence section composed of two thick, roughly hewn horizontal rails connected by three vertical posts. The wood appears weathered and dark, with visible grain and slight irregularities, suggesting it is hand-crafted or aged. The ends of the rails are blunt and uneven, and the posts are squared off with no decorative caps. There are no visible fasteners or embellishments, giving the fence a utilitarian, rural appearance. The structure is open, with significant gaps between the rails, making it more of a boundary marker than a security barrier. This type of fence is commonly found in parks, gardens, or along pathways in urban green spaces.
description: A rustic, two-rail wooden fence section with three vertical posts and a weathered, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fence, with two horizontal rails and three vertical posts clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the fence, with the posts and rails aligned and minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the two rails and three posts from the other side.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, again showing the thin profile and the alignment of the rails and posts.
